title: Control interface
abstract: In one aspect, a method for adjusting one or more parameters using an interface will be described. A contact point on a display is detected. A parameter is adjusted based on a distance between a reference point and the contact point. A second parameter is adjusted based on an angle between a base line and a contact line. The base line is a reference line that extends from the reference point. The contact line is a reference line that connects the contact point with the reference point. Various systems, devices and software are described that help implement the above method.
claims: Claims ( 15 ) A method for adjusting at least two parameters using a graphical user interface, the method comprising: detecting a first contact point on a touch sensitive display coupled with a device; adjusting a first parameter based on a distance between a reference point on the display and the first contact point; adjusting a second parameter based on an angle between a first base line and a first contact line wherein: the first base line is a reference line on the display that extends from the reference point; and the first contact line is a reference line on the display that connects the first contact point with the reference point. The method of claim 1 wherein: the device is an oven; the first parameter is temperature; the second parameter is time; and the method further comprises: raising a temperature of air in a heating compartment of the oven to the adjusted temperature; and setting a timer based on the adjusted time. The method of claim 1 further comprising: detecting a second contact point on the touch sensitive display; and based on the second contact point, determining a third parameter. The method of claim 1 further comprising: displaying a graphical interface including a plurality of sections including a first section, each section covering a different range of angles around the reference point wherein: the plurality of sections is associated with a plurality of different features for the device, respectively; the first section is associated with a first feature of the plurality of features; the first contact point is positioned within the first section; the first base line is at a periphery of the first section; and the first and second parameters are control parameters for the first feature. The method of claim 4 further comprising: detecting a second contact point on the touch sensitive display wherein the second contact point is within a second section of the plurality of sections, the second section being associated with a second feature of the plurality of features; adjusting a third parameter based on a distance between the reference point and the second contact point; adjusting a fourth parameter based on an angle between a second base line and a second contact line wherein: the second base line is a reference line on the display that extends from the reference point and that is at a periphery of the second section; and the second contact line is a reference line on the display that connects the second contact point with the reference point. The method of claim 4 wherein the device is a microwave oven and the plurality of features associated with the plurality of sections each involve one of 1) a general cooking mode; 2) a defrost mode; 3) a pizza cooking mode; 4) a meat cooking mode; 5) a popcorn cooking mode; and 5) a reheat mode. The method of claim 1 further comprising: displaying at least part of a circular region whose radius changes in length depending on the distance between the reference point and the first contact point. The method of claim 1 further comprising: displaying a shaded region whose borders are defined at least in part by the contact line and the base line. The method of claim 1 further comprising: detecting a second contact point on the touch sensitive display; adjusting a third parameter based on a distance between the reference point and the second contact point; adjusting a fourth parameter based on an angle between the first base line and a second contact line wherein the second contact line is a reference line on the display that connects the second contact point with the reference point. The method of claim 9 wherein: the device is an oven; the first parameter is a first temperature; the second parameter is a first time; the third parameter is a second temperature; the fourth parameter is a second time; the method further comprises: raising a temperature of a first heating compartment of the oven to the adjusted first temperature; heating the first heating compartment for a time period based on the adjusted first time; raising a temperature of a second heating compartment of the oven to the adjusted second temperature; and heating the second heating compartment for a time period based on the adjusted second time. The method of claim 1 further comprising: displaying a contact marker, the contact marker indicating a location of the first contact point on the display; moving the contact marker when the contact marker is touched and dragged by the user wherein the adjustment of the first and second parameters is based on the movement of the contact marker. The method of claim 1 wherein the second parameter is a time period, the method further comprising: detecting that the first contact point has moved 360Â° around the reference point; and in response to the detection of the 360Â° movement of the first contact point, adding 60 minutes to the time period. The method of claim 12 further comprising: in response to the detection of the 360Â° movement of the first contact point, displaying a circle around the reference point to indicate the 360Â° movement and the addition of 60 minutes. A computer readable storage medium including computer readable code embodied in a tangible form wherein the computer readable code comprises: executable computer code for detecting a first contact point on a touch sensitive display coupled with a device; executable computer code for adjusting a first parameter based on a distance between a reference point on the display and the first contact point; and executable computer code for adjusting a second parameter based on an angle between a first base line and a first contact line wherein: the first base line is a reference line on the display that connects to and extends from the reference point; and the first contact line is a reference line on the display that connects the first contact point with the reference point. A device comprising: a touch sensitive display; at least one processor; at least one memory including a computer readable storage medium that includes computer code stored in a tangible form wherein the computer code, when executed by the at least one processor, causes the device to: detect a first contact point on the touch sensitive display; adjust a first parameter based on a distance between a reference point on the display and the first contact point; and adjust a second parameter based on an angle between a first base line and a first contact line that are connected at the reference point wherein: the first base line is a reference line on the display that extends from the reference point; and the first contact line is a reference line on the display that connects the first contact point with the reference point.
